selected_hotels.first_review: What makes a good hotel great? The people of course! La Siesta has a great team of staff who make your stay a memorable one! They are attentive, friendly & so supportive! I would thoroughly recommend staying here. The location is terrific, the restaurant one of the best in Hanoi. Special mention to Chille, Hằng, Duk, Chuong & Tuan! We will definitely be back! Missing you already!

The most beautiful hotel with the best service. We enjoyed our stay at Sofitel Metropole and came and went three times over our Vietnam holiday. The location is excellent and places the hotel in easy walking distance to many restaurants, the lake and shopping, including the old quarter. The service in this hotel is outstanding with checkin and checkout very smooth with all staff wanting to assist whenever they could. The Metropole is very beautiful with french architecture and design visible everywhere. Gorgeous lighting and ornate wall features and doorways. There is a lovely pool area and a courtyard garden on the first floor. Breakfast is a massive selection of food to suit every nationality and all so nicely presented. We took a stroll around the lake on a Friday night when the streets were closed off and this was one of the highlights of Hanoi for us, a touch of culture and a warm evening with Vietnamese families all relaxing and enjoying the space, music and atmosphere. …

Had a great time there in Bespoke Trendy Hotel, room was clean, its in a convenient area, breakfast was good and the staff were really friendly. In particular, David who was amazing and really went out of the way to make sure we were safe and taken care of through our tour in Hanoi. 10/10 would recommend.

Being near food and attractions, this conveniently located euro-styled boutique hotel was a gem to stay for my recent anniversary. With spacious & comfortable bedrooms plus boasting of a reputable restuarant (ESSENCE) that offers a grand mix menu of International and local cuisines , it also provides solace from the heat of the day should one decide to stay indoors. Special mention to Tony and his team who were always attentive to details. Being patient and understanding, he was always on hand to address our concerns and provided us with quickfix solutions. Highly recommended for travellers looking to experience Vietnamese hospitality; Looking forward to a return stay soon.

Your team gave me a souvenir for my 100th night, but I estimate I have stayed a total of >130 nights over the last ten years. There is not enough space to write all the good things about Nikko Saigon, so I will limit it to three: 1. Nikko combines the Japanese way of meticulous attention to detail and the Vietnamese way of kindness, unpretentiousness, and flexibility into an art form. This level of authentic service exceeds that of Imperial Hotel Tokyo or Hyatt or Intercontinental in Saigon. 2. During COVID, it offered peace of mind by rigorously enforcing hygiene standards and providing extraordinary assistance to guests who were COVID-positive. I recount that I was seriously sick in the hotel in 2022 and I recalled they did everything they could to help. For example, they gave me so much food from the breakfast counter that I was fully stocked for all my meals. Anything that I want they will help bring to the door ASAP. Every effort was made to assist me deal with medical tests and other logistical arrangements. 3. They just go above and beyond. On multiple occasions, sushi run out at the executive lounge, and if I remotely glanced at the empty plate, an associate would somehow arrange "custom order" for me (for free) even if I don't ask. Once I broke my suitcase and I didn't have cash, they literally have the suitcase fixed at a shop nearby and then paid for me. It was not a huge amount of money but it is the gesture that counts. Once I lost something, the lost and found department will pack it so well and so carefully that when I got it back, it literally looked like a gift to me. I have made similar feedback to a former GM long ago but it is only appropriate and deserving that I articulate my comments in writing publicly. Thank you.…

The location was perfect and right in the centre of the old town, making it easy for us to walk everywhere. The staff were super friendly and Hana was in regular contact a while before our stay, to help us organise everything we needed. On arrival they gave us a long list of recommendations for Hanoi, and important things to keep an eye out for, as it was our first time in Vietnam. The hotel was clean and comfortable, and was exactly what we were hoping for our stay!

Good location as there are many places to go around. The only set back was that the pool could not be used. Hence we would need to use the other Liberty hotel, ie riverside. Great team there, especially one of the doorman, Phong. Good service ! One thing to note, if you are taking the non city view, please ask for higher floor. We were originally given the 6th floor but windows open to a building next to us. Went to ask for a change and they moved us to 1009 which had a better view.
Such a lovely bouquet hotel, clean, good foods with a great service mind. it is located in the foods and shopping area. Very convenient and easy to go everywhere. If I've chance, I will revisit sure!

The staff at O Gallery are very friendly and made me feel like an A-list celebrity, especially the receptionist Bora. She double-checked with my tour company that they were picking me up for my tour to Ha Long Bay and they let me keep my extra luggage there while I went on my side trip. The room was clean and very quiet. I had just been traveling for a month and it was nice to be able to have a night in and watch HBO. The breakfast was one of the best I have had while traveling. I also want to mention the spa because it is worth it! I decided to treat myself and I did the Deluxe Package which was about 4 hours of spa treatments (massage, body scrub, facial). Huong gave me the best massage of my life, I came out of my appointment feeling like every muscle in my body was relaxed and my skin was glowing. After a month of sun and sweating it was great to exfoliate and rehydrate. …

The Hotel is located is a very nice area, everything just walking distance to locals treats and Mall. The Staff is the best! Breakfast was delicious and has many options with the locals food. I would recommended to stay here while visit Saigon.
